psx.notes: 172 games. .m3u files live in sonyplaystation/Favorite/ with relative paths ..\images\GameName\disc.cue pointing to sonyplaystation/images/. All .7z archives extracted and deleted. Images moved to images/ to prevent ES double-detection.

dreamcast.notes: 55 games. .m3u files in segadreamcast/Favorite/ with relative paths GameName\disc.cue. Disc images remain in Favorite/GameName/ subfolders — no loose disc files at Favorite root so no ES double-detection issue.

learned.note: PSX Favorite folder was initially organized as named '<game>.m3u' directories containing .7z archives and an inner .m3u file. The user's faux_m3u_dirs policy was forbid, so required repair was: extract .7z archives (7z.exe via WSL with wslpath -w and < /dev/null), rename folders to remove .m3u extension, move .m3u to Favorite root, then relocate all image folders to sonyplaystation/images/ to prevent ES double-detection.

Dreamcast Favorite folder had the same '<game>.m3u' directory structure as PSX. Images were already extracted (no .7z). Fixed by: extracting inner .m3u content, renaming folders (remove .m3u extension), writing new .m3u files at Favorite root with GameName\disc.cue relative paths.

7-Zip installed at C:/Program Files/7-Zip/7z.exe (WSL: /mnt/c/Program Files/7-Zip/7z.exe). Requires wslpath -w for all path arguments. Always redirect stdin with < /dev/null when calling 7z inside bash while-read loops to prevent stdin consumption.

Final Fantasy Anthology Vol.1/2, Final Fantasy Chronicles, Final Fantasy Origins had .bin files only (no .cue). Generated MODE2/2352 .cue files manually. Format: FILE "game.bin" BINARY / TRACK 01 MODE2/2352 / INDEX 01 00:00:00

Legend of Dragoon: single .7z contained all 4 disc images in a subfolder with .iso format (not .cue). .m3u was updated to reference 4 .iso files directly. Arc the Lad archives used full collection title in filenames (Arc the Lad Collection - Arc the Lad (USA)) despite .7z being named Arc the Lad (USA).7z.
